- Row 0 inner: (5,13) B, (5,23) B, (5,36) B, (5,46) B - the actual pattern blocks
- Row 1: (13,12) S, (13,22) P, (13,35) S, (13,45) P
- Row 1 inner: (14,13) B, (14,23) B, (14,36) B, (14,46) B
- Row 2: (22,12) S, (22,22) P, (22,35) S, (22,45) P
- Row 2 inner: (23,13) B, (23,23) B, (23,36) B, (23,46) B
So each row has:
- Background blocks: S (sky blue) and P (pink) - these form the colored squares
- Inner blocks: B (black) - these are the patterns inside
The bottom blue blocks are at cols 15, 22, 29, 36, 43. Only col 22 matches top blue at (23, 13).
Wait, (23, 13) is the blue block in the bottom row of the top panel, at column 13 (which is the left blue block).

sections.content:
World model: Blue blocks don't cycle with UP/DOWN. Only 1/5 bottom blue blocks matches a top blue block. Goal may require matching bottom blue blocks to specific top blue blocks, or matching pink blocks to specific (not any) top pink blocks.
Plan: Test if LEFT/RIGHT cycles blue blocks when arrow is on blue row.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check arrow position first
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        mr = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        mc = min(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        print(f"Arrow at ({mr}, {mc})")

# Record bottom blue hashes
blue_before = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        mr = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        mc = min(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        if 40 <= mr <= 45:
            blue_before[mc] = n['hash']

# Try LEFT to see if blue blocks cycle
action(['LEFT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
blue_after = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B':
        mr = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        mc = min(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        if 40 <= mr <= 45:
            blue_after[mc] = n['hash']

changed = False
for col in sorted(blue_before.keys()):
    if blue_before[col] != blue_after.get(col, 'N/A'):
        changed = True
        print(f"Blue col {col}: {blue_before[col][:12]} -> {blue_after.get(col, 'N/A')[:12]}")

if not changed:
    print("Blue blocks unchanged by LEFT")

# Check arrow position after
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        mr = min(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])
        mc = min(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])
        print(f"Arrow now at ({mr}, {mc})")

print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
